title: Chapter 21 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)
content:

{{Quote|Picking up where Volume I left off, this episode begins on the battle-scarred world of Hypori, in the wreckage of a crashed Republic starship. Inside, General Grievous continues to cut through a team of trapped Jedi warriors. It's up to a crack unit of heavily armed ARC troopers to come to the rescue.|StarWars.com summary|<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/21.html|text=''Clone Wars'': Chapter Twenty-One (small)}}</ref>}}
"'''Chapter 21'''" is the twenty-first episode of the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' animated television series, and the first episode in [[Clone Wars: Season Three|Season Three]] and [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume Two|Volume Two]]. The episode originally aired on [[Cartoon Network]] on [[March 21]], [[2005]].

==Plot summary==
A customized [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry]] speeds across the surface of [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]]. On board the [[Gunship/Legends|gunship]], a squad of [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commandos]] prepare their weapons for attack. A jamming signal prevents their detection by an army of [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]] surrounding a crashed [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]]. The gunship drops a cluster of [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]], armed on standby, among the [[Droid/Legends|droids]].

[[File:GrievousHypori.jpg|thumb|left|Grievous attacking the clones.]]
The troopers disembark, their commander—[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Fordo]]—detecting three life signs. Two of the ARC troopers go after the nearest two, while the rest follow Fordo to the third reading; the [[Transport/Legends|transport]] is flown skywards to open fire on the approaching [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. As the troopers exit a tunnel, they encounter [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], who is in the midst of a vicious [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] duel with [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]]. Just as he disarms Mundi and prepares to finish him off, they both notice the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clones]], who unleash a barrage of [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire at the [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]]. Grievous flees the attack, climbing up the wall and onto the ceiling. As Grievous leaps down and quickly [[Death/Legends|kills]] four of the troopers using two of his lightsabers, the commander calls for backup. The hovering LAAT/i breaks into the area and opens fire on the General with its blasters and missiles, which he evades.
{{Quote|No, we must pursue!|Ki-Adi Mundi|<ref name="Episode">{{CW|21}}</ref>}}
[[File:Kkruhksaber.jpg|thumb|right|Grievous claiming [[K'Kruhk]]'s lightsaber.]]
Fordo orders his men to retreat, despite Ki-Adi-Mundi's initial insistence that they pursue Grievous. Two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]] and [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]], have survived the attack by General Grievous and are recovering aboard the LAAT/i. Meanwhile, Grievous takes a lightsaber from the seemingly lifeless body of [[K'Kruhk]] and adds it to his collection.

The ARC troopers detonate the explosives, destroying hundreds of droids. General Grievous watches the transport head into space, musing that they have only prolonged their downfall…
{{Quote|Run, Jedi, run. You have only prolonged the inevitable.|Grievous|<ref name="Episode" />}}
On [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] has a vision of the nine-year-old [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and the late [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] on [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]]. Qui-Gon tells Skywalker he must face his fear by entering the [[Cave of Evil/Legends|Dark Side Cave]], and to trust in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].

At a meeting of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], Ki-Adi-Mundi tells his fellow council-members of the [[Battle of Hypori/Legends|defeat on Hypori]]. Since the Jedi numbers are spread so thinly, the Council decide to promote Anakin Skywalker to full [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] status at [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] request, despite protests from Master [[Oppo Rancisis/Legends|Oppo Rancisis]].

{{Quote|This is preposterous! We can't set aside our most hallowed traditions.|[[Oppo Rancisis/Legends|Oppo Rancisis]]|<ref name="Episode" />}}
Skywalker walks through crowds amid the lower levels of Coruscant, surveying the surrounding people with suspicion. He thinks a hooded figure is following him, so he races off. At the entrance to an alleyway, he grabs the mysterious figure and ignites his lightsaber before realizing his pursuer is his beloved wife, [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]. They share a kiss and, thinking they have been seen by a passerby, Skywalker rants that they should not have to hide their love. Amidala reminds him of his duty to the Jedi Order and that she'll always love him. He jokes that Amidala ''does'' look good in the dark and they kiss again, though this time they are interrupted by [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], who is clad in brand-new, gold-colored coverings. Skywalker claims to be impressed; ''most'' impressed.

Skywalker is called by Kenobi to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], but he arrives late, interpreting his being summoned as a prelude to another scolding. Kenobi reminds his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] that he is no longer a child but must still follow his Master's orders. Skywalker retorts that Kenobi is "no Qui-Gon Jinn."; this saddens Kenobi visibly and Skywalker immediately regrets his hurtful comment, apologizing. Master Kenobi forgives his student thus confessing that he misses their old friend and mentor Qui-Gon too and then shows him to a dark room, where Kenobi and the other [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] surround Skywalker and ignite their lightsabers.

{{Quote|Step forward, Padawan. Anakin Skywalker, by the right of the Council, by the will of the Force, dub thee I do, Jedi... Knight of the Republic.|Yoda|<ref name="Episode" />}}
Yoda [[Knighting Ceremony/Legends|knights]] Skywalker, cutting off his [[Padawan braid/Legends|Padawan braid]], which C-3PO later presents to Amidala, which she happily stores away with her [[Japor snippet/Legends|Japor snippet]] for safekeeping. In return, Amidala gives [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] to Skywalker, the droid showing the new [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knight]] a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] recording of Amidala presenting him the gift and accepting his own. Skywalker smiles at the sight and reaches into the hologram with his [[Mechno-arm/Legends|mechno-arm]] as if to hold her in his hand. He and R2-D2 then board [[Anakin Skywalker's Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|his]] [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|''Actis''-class]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] and leave once again to serve together in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]…

==Continuity==
[[File:JediHighCouncilCloneWars.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Jedi High Council discussing the Knighthood of Anakin Skywalker. Initially placed after the [[Battle of Hypori/Legends|Battle of Hypori]], this event was moved to occur after the [[Mission to Vjun (Clone Wars)|Mission to Vjun]] as to not contradict the novel ''[[Jedi Trial]]''.]]
There are several [[easter egg]]s during the Coruscant street scene: an animated version of [[Genndy Tartakovsky]], a creature resembling "The Creeper" from the [[Wikipedia:Scooby-Doo|''Scooby-Doo'']] cartoons, the [[Battlestarwiki:Cylons (TOS)|Cylon]] [[Battlestarwiki:Centurion (TOS)|Centurion]] from the original [[Wikipedia:Battlestar Galactica|''Battlestar Galactica'']] and a "Woolie" from Tartakovky's series ''Samurai Jack''.<ref name="Comm">[[Genndy Tartakovsky|Tartakovsky, Genndy]]. (2005). Commentary for "Chapter 21", in ''Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume II'' [DVD]. [[Lucasfilm]].</ref> When C-3PO reveals his new golden plating to Anakin, a [[Music/Legends|musical]] cue of "stripper music" can be heard.<ref name="Comm" /> In the same scene, Anakin's line "Impressive. Most impressive." mimics the same line he says (as [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]) [[3 ABY/Legends|over two decades later]] to his son [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] (in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] ).<ref name="Comm" />

When Genndy Tartakovsky and team began working on this episode, [[George Lucas]]'s first instruction to the crew was to resolve the [[Hypori/Legends|Hypori]] storyline which the crew had begun in "[[Chapter 20 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 20]]." He specified this because his son [[Jett Lucas|Jett]] was eager to find out how that particular story ends, as there were originally no plans to continue the series beyond "Chapter 20." The unique designs on the front of the clones' [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAATs]] were inspired by [[Wikipedia:Nose art|nose art]] seen on military aircraft. According to [[Paul Rudish]] on the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume Two|Volume Two]] DVD commentary, the front of the LAAT featured at the beginning of this episode was meant to look like the face of a [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]]. The clones' customizations of their helmets in this episode and in the rest of Volume Two is based on real-life actions done by soldiers during World War II and the [[Wikipedia:Vietnam War|Vietnam War]].

According to the commentary for "[[Chapter 3 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 3]]," the creators had studied how special forces teams acted in real combat situations, to ensure the ARC troopers acted very much like a crack special forces team would. They apparently reapplied their knowledge for this episode (Captain Fordo used hand signals instead of actually speaking, and as they approach Ki-Adi-Mundi and General Grievous he directed members to perform what appear to be flanking maneuvers). According to the episode commentary, Ki-Adi-Mundi's concern for Shaak Ti was intended to hint at a possible relationship between the two. Most of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species in the [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Mos Eisley Cantina]] scenes in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] appear in the underlevels of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

In the series, Anakin is depicted as being knighted very early on (while his hair is still short) as a response to a shortage of Jedi Knights, and believing that Anakin was long overdue for knighthood. This seemingly conflicts with the novel ''[[Jedi Trial]]'' by [[David Sherman]] and [[Dan Cragg]], in which Anakin, only a few months prior to [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']], is sent on a mission to earn his knighthood. However, [[Leland Chee]] did confirm that there is in fact a jump in time between the conclusion of the Hypori battle and the Jedi Council scenes leading up to Anakin's knighting, claiming Anakin's hair was in a top-knot that he merely let down after being knighted.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=196444&start=119#9996211|thread=EU Leadup to Revenge of the Sith|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2005-03-21|time=4:41 PM}}</ref>

Although [[K'Kruhk]] appears to have been killed by Grievous in "[[Chapter 20 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|Chapter 20]]," and is not rescued with the other Jedi, he is actually known to have survived this battle and is in a state of hibernation when Grievous takes his lightsaber. Although Eeth Koth remained canonically dead after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], a [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] member that appears in the Council scene, officially credited as [[Agen Kolar/Legends|Agen Kolar]], was animated instead with the likeness of [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]]. It is unknown if the character was originally intended to be Eeth Koth (in ignorance of his canonical death) and then retroactively credited as Agen Kolar to avoid a continuity problem, or if the Zabrak was always intended to be Kolar and the animators accidentally used the wrong reference photographs.
